title: gochnatia polymorpha subsp. ceanothifolia
intro: Gochnatia polymorpha subsp. ceanothifolia, also known as Huacacolla, is a plant species native to the Andean region, specifically in Peru and Bolivia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Gochnatia polymorpha subsp. ceanothifolia include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to treat digestive issues and reduce stress levels. Herbal preparations of this plant are often used in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
